title: Vector Page Builder: Intranet-Style Custom Dashboards & Container Model Architecture
topic: Custom page builder for team/product intranet dashboards — drag-drop composition, data binding, multi-level views (team, product owner, executive)
summary: Vector becomes a full intranet platform. Page builder with Elementor-style hierarchical container model (Section → Row → Column → Widget) enables flexible, responsive dashboard composition backed by real-time Git, work items, and metrics. Standalone component architecture behind paywall.
content:
Contents
  1. Vision & Scope
  2. Elementor vs. Vector: Feature Parity
  3. Decomposition Strategy: 100 Stories Across 6 Phases
  4. Architecture Deep-Dive: Container Model
  5. Hierarchical Element Structure
  6. Build Phases (5 Phases)
  7. User Stories & Backlog
  8. Data Model & Schema
  9. Widget Library (Reusable Components)
  10. Sources

Vision & Scope

Goal: Transform Vector into a full intranet platform where teams and stakeholders build custom dashboards and branded spaces backed by unified, real-time data (Git, work items, deployments, cycle time).

Three levels of pages:

  • Team intranet: Team members drag-drop widgets (Git graph, PR status, cycle time) to visualize their own work.
  • Product owner view: PO sees aggregated progress across multiple teams on a single dashboard.
  • Executive dashboard: Exec sees product-wide health (release status, risk, forecasts) at a glance.

Core insight: Pages are not a closed system. Any widget (Git graph, commit feed, deployment status) built for Git integration (R016 stories 00171–00174) can be embedded into custom pages. The page builder is the composition layer; Git widgets are the building blocks.

Standalone architecture: PageBuilder is a paywalled, self-contained component tree with its own namespace and UI surface, apart from the database (which is shared with core Vector). No PageBuilder code in the main app router or global nav—all code lives within dedicated component structure.

SharePoint-like vision: Each team gets branded, custom-built intranet space. But unlike SharePoint, all data is live, queryable, and backed by Vector's unified schema (work items, Git, deployments, cycle time).

Elementor vs. Vector: Feature Parity Table

To clarify scope, here is what Elementor ships with and what we will—and won't—build:

RefFeature CategoryElementor (Full Suite)Vector PageBuilder (Phase 1–5)Vector: Won't Build (Out of Scope)Notes
F-01Container ModelSection → Container → Column → Widget✓ Section → Row → Column → WidgetN/AVector uses Row instead of Container; same semantics
F-02Responsive BreakpointsDesktop, Tablet, Mobile per-element overrides✓ Desktop (1200), Tablet (768), Mobile (375)N/AIdentical approach: breakpoints object in JSON
F-03Drag-to-Nest✓ Drag widget/container to any container✓ Drag widget → column, column → row, row → sectionN/AVector: leaf widgets only (no nested containers)
F-04Preset Layouts✓ ~50+ pre-built templates✓ 4 presets (1/2/3/4-col sections)Advanced presets (carousel, masonry, custom grids)Vector ships minimal (MVP); can extend later
F-05Widget Library✓ 80+ widgets (image, video, button, icon, carousel, form, etc.)✓ 8 widgets (git-graph, pr-status, cycle-time, deployment, work-item-list, burndown, risk-summary, metric-gauge)General-purpose widgets (form fields, video, gallery, social embeds)Vector ships dashboard-specific; no forms/media yet
F-06Data Binding / Live DataDynamic tags only; no real-time subscriptions✓ Data source registry + WebSocket subscriptions (Phase 3)N/AVector's key differentiator: live-updating dashboards
F-07Theme Editor✓ Colors, fonts, global styles, CSS variables✓ Brand color, logo, font family, CSS variables (Phase 5)N/AVector: simpler theme system (no global style inheritance yet)
F-08Forms & Input Widgets✓ Form builder, text input, select, checkbox, file upload✗Form widgets (text, select, checkbox, file, email)Vector PageBuilder is view-only (read-only dashboards)
F-09Media Widgets✓ Image, video, audio, icon, lottie, map✗ (Image, logo only)Video, audio, map, embedded contentPhase 1–5 is dashboard-focused; media later
F-10Advanced Containers✓ Tabs, accordion, carousel, repeater (dynamic groups)✗ (Repeater planned for Phase 3+)Tabs, accordion, carousel (MVP excludes; future feature)Row is fixed container; Tabs/Accordion in Phase 3+ roadmap
F-11Text & Typography Controls✓ Heading, paragraph, text editor (WYSIWYG), rich text✓ Text widget (read-only labels, titles)WYSIWYG editor, inline text editingVector: static text only; no rich editing in builder
F-12Advanced Styling✓ Box shadow, border, gradient, hover effects, animations✓ Background color, padding, gap, basic stylingGradients, shadows, animations, custom CSSVector: MVP styling only; advanced FX phase 2+
F-13Export / Import✓ JSON export, template library, cross-site import✓ Page JSON export, duplication, sharing (Phase 5)N/AVector: JSON export for version control + team sharing
F-14Access Control / Permissions✓ Role-based (pro), template sharing, team workspaces✓ Page-level ownership + team sharing (Phase 5)Fine-grained role inheritance, widget-level permissionsVector: page-level only; no widget-level ACL
F-15Repeaters / Dynamic Content✓ Repeater field (loop over dynamic data)△ Planned (Phase 3+ roadmap)N/AVector: future feature for multi-team/multi-repo iteration
F-16Conditions / Visibility Rules✓ Show/hide elements based on dynamic tags△ Element visibility toggle per breakpoint (Phase 4)Advanced conditional logic (data-based show/hide)Vector Phase 1–5: no conditional rendering yet
F-17Undo / Redo✓ Full history with snapshots✓ Immer-based undo/redo (Phase 1)N/AVector: up to 50 steps; Immer-managed
F-18Collaboration✗ (No real-time co-editing)✗Real-time co-editing, conflict resolution, version controlOut of scope for Phase 1–5; future feature
F-19Template Marketplace✓ Elementor marketplace with 1000s of templates✗Template marketplace, community templatesVector: internal presets only; no marketplace
F-20A/B Testing & Analytics✗ (Via integration with 3rd-party plugins)✗A/B testing, page analytics, conversion trackingOut of scope; can integrate later if needed
F-21Template Creation & Library✓ Template library, save as template, template reuse✓ Page templates + component templates (Phase 5)N/ATeams create and share full-page templates and custom component templates across tenant; templates are discoverable and versioned

Summary: Vector PageBuilder is a focused, data-driven dashboard builder—not a general website builder. Priorities: live data binding, team/PO/exec scoping, theme customization, export/sharing. We skip forms, media, advanced containers, marketplace, and collaboration in Phase 1–5.

Decomposition Strategy: 100 Stories Across 6 Phases

The 14-story outline above is insufficient for a feature this scale. Vector PageBuilder encompasses backend infrastructure, frontend canvas, real-time subscriptions, multiple widget implementations, responsive design, theme management, and team scoping. A proper decomposition spans ~100 stories split across 6 phases with clear dependencies and shippable milestones.

Decomposition Rubric

One story = one observable, shippable unit. A user (or developer) can see it is done without knowing about sibling stories.

Split rules:

  • Backend ≠ Frontend: API endpoint + frontend call are separate stories.
  • Migration ≠ Handler: DB table creation is a separate story from the handler that uses it.
  • Each container type: Section, Row, Column — each gets its own render/drag/config story.
  • Each widget: Each built-in widget is its own story.
  • Render ≠ Edit: View-mode rendering and edit-mode canvas are separate.
  • Config ≠ Binding: Properties panel (styling) and data source binding are separate.
  • Static ≠ Realtime: Static data display and WebSocket live update are separate.
  • Paywall: Feature gate is its own story.

Story EST targets (Fibonacci F0–F13):

  • F2–F3: Pure DB migration, single API endpoint, single UI component with no state complexity.
  • F5: Multi-step backend service + handler, or a feature-rich UI component.
  • F8: Complex UI feature (drag-to-nest, responsive editor, properties panel).
  • F13: Full subsystem (WebSocket infrastructure, widget registry, template library).
  • No F21+: Auto-split into F13 or smaller.

Layer prefix for titles (for scanability):

  • [DB] — migration only
  • [BE] — backend service + handler
  • [FE] — frontend component or feature
  • [API] — lib function in app/lib/
  • [E2E] — integration or end-to-end test story
  • [INFRA] — paywall gate, routing, scaffolding

Phase Structure (6 Phases, ~100 Stories)

Phase 0 — Foundation & Scaffolding (10 stories)

Establish the standalone PageBuilder namespace, paywall gate, routing, DB tables, and base backend package. No visible UI yet — just the skeleton that everything else builds on.

  1. [DB] PageBuilder layout table migration (page_builder_layouts)
  2. [DB] PageBuilder bindings table migration (page_builder_bindings)
  3. [DB] Data sources registry table migration (page_builder_data_sources)
  4. [DB] PageBuilder templates table migration (page_builder_templates)
  5. [BE] Backend package scaffold — service + handler stubs, route registration in main.go
  6. [INFRA] Frontend routing — add page-builder PageId, lazy-loaded entry point, PageShell wrapper
  7. [INFRA] Paywall gate — role/subscription check for PageBuilder access; locked state UI
  8. [BE] Page CRUD — List, Get, Create, Delete endpoints (no layout yet)
  9. [API] Frontend lib — app/lib/pageBuilder.ts with listPages, getPage, createPage, deletePage
  10. [FE] Page list & creation UI — list of user's pages, "New Page" modal (label + icon picker)

Phase 1 — Canvas & Container Hierarchy (18 stories)

Render the element tree on a canvas. Section → Row → Column structure is interactive and serializes to JSON. No widgets yet — containers only.

  1. [BE] Layout save/load endpoints — GET /api/pagebuilder/{id}/layout, POST /api/pagebuilder/{id}/layout
  2. [API] Frontend lib — getLayout, saveLayout calls
  3. [FE] Immer-based canvas state — useCanvasState hook: element tree, selection, dirty flag, undo/redo stack
  4. [FE] Section render — renders a Section container on the canvas with visual chrome (border, label, padding)
  5. [FE] Row render — renders a Row with N equal-width Columns inside a Section
  6. [FE] Column render — renders a Column cell inside a Row (flex-sized, populated/empty states)
  7. [FE] Element selection — click to select any element; highlights selected element; shows selection ring
  8. [FE] Section drag reorder — drag a Section up/down within the page; siblings reflow
  9. [FE] Row drag reorder — drag a Row up/down within a Section
  10. [FE] Column drag reorder — drag a Column left/right within a Row; siblings reflow
  11. [FE] Add Section — button/toolbar to add a new blank Section to the page
  12. [FE] Add Row — add a Row inside a selected Section (choose column count 1–6)
  13. [FE] Delete element — delete any selected element (and its children) with confirmation
  14. [FE] Undo/redo UI — Undo/Redo toolbar buttons wired to useCanvasState
  15. [FE] Canvas empty state — illustrated empty state when page has no Sections; prompt to add first Section
  16. [BE] Layout PATCH endpoint — partial update (individual element settings) without full replace
  17. [API] Frontend lib — patchLayout call
  18. [E2E] Canvas hierarchy integration test — create Section → Row (2-col) → verify JSON serialization

Phase 2 — Properties Panel (12 stories)

Context-aware right-sidebar properties panel. Each container type has its own property set.

  1. [FE] Properties panel shell — right sidebar, slides in when element selected, collapses when nothing selected
  2. [FE] Section properties — width (boxed/full-width), max-width (px), padding (top/right/bottom/left), background colour, gap
  3. [FE] Row properties — column count (1–6), gap between columns, vertical alignment (top/middle/bottom/stretch)
  4. [FE] Column properties — width override (% or flex), padding, minimum height
  5. [FE] Section background image — upload image as background for a Section (stored as base64 in JSON)
  6. [FE] Element visibility toggle — show/hide toggle per element (hides from view mode, still in JSON)
  7. [FE] Properties auto-save — debounced save to layout JSON on every property change (500ms debounce)
  8. [FE] Duplicate element — duplicate any selected element (deep clone with new IDs)
  9. [BE] Element settings validation — server-side validation of settings shapes in layout PATCH
  10. [FE] Copy/paste element — copy selected element, paste as sibling below (cross-section paste)
  11. [FE] Element label editor — inline rename any element (stored in label field, visible in panel only)
  12. [E2E] Properties panel integration test — select Row, change column count to 3, verify canvas reflows

Phase 3 — Palette & Presets (8 stories)

Left sidebar palette for adding containers and widgets. Preset layout buttons for rapid scaffolding.

  1. [FE] Palette shell — left sidebar with two tabs: Containers / Widgets
  2. [FE] Container palette items — drag Section, Row, Column from palette onto canvas
  3. [FE] Drag from palette to canvas — drop Section onto page, Row onto Section, Column onto Row
  4. [FE] Preset 1-col — insert a full-width Section with 1-column Row
  5. [FE] Preset 2-col — insert a Section with equal 2-column Row
  6. [FE] Preset 3-col — insert a Section with equal 3-column Row
  7. [FE] Preset 4-col — insert a Section with equal 4-column Row
  8. [FE] Palette search — filter palette items by name; highlights matches

Phase 4 — Widget Registry & Core Widgets (22 stories)

Widget component registry, widget scaffold, and 8 built-in widgets. Each widget ships with its own properties in the panel. Mock data only — no live data binding yet.

  1. [BE] Widget registry backend — GET /api/pagebuilder/widgets returns widget manifest list (type, name, icon, configSchema)
  2. [API] Frontend lib — listWidgets call
  3. [FE] Widget registry frontend — widgetRegistry.ts mapping type → component + manifest
  4. [FE] Widget placeholder render — any widget renders a placeholder card (icon, name, "Not configured") when binding is absent
  5. [FE] Widget properties panel section — generic widget panel section: height, title override, border, background
  6. [FE] Add Widget — drag widget from palette into a Column; inserts with default settings
  7. [FE] Widget: Git Graph — commit DAG with branch lanes, mock data, config: repos (list), show branches, show deployments
  8. [FE] Widget: PR Status Feed — scrollable PR list (title, author, status badge), mock data, config: repos
  9. [FE] Widget: Cycle Time Forecast — forecast bar chart with confidence band, mock data, config: time window, confidence level
  10. [FE] Widget: Deployment Timeline — horizontal timeline of deployments per environment, mock data, config: environment filter
  11. [FE] Widget: Work Item List — filterable list with status chips, mock data, config: item types, status filter
  12. [FE] Widget: Burndown Chart — sprint burndown line chart (ideal vs actual), mock data, config: sprint selector
  13. [FE] Widget: Risk Summary — red/amber/green card grid, mock data, config: risk threshold levels
  14. [FE] Widget: Metric Gauge — single-value gauge (arc), mock data, config: metric key, thresholds, unit
  15. [FE] Widget: Text — static rich-text label (markdown rendered), config: content string, font size, alignment
  16. [FE] Widget: Divider — horizontal rule with spacing, config: thickness, colour, margin
  17. [FE] Widget palette items — all 10 widgets appear in palette Widgets tab with icons and names
  18. [BE] Widget config validation — server-side validation of widget settings in layout save
  19. [FE] Widget resize handle — drag bottom edge of widget to set explicit height; stores in settings.height
  20. [E2E] Widget registry integration test — drag Git Graph widget into column, verify JSON structure
  21. [E2E] Widget render test — each widget renders without error with mock data (snapshot suite)
  22. [FE] Widget error boundary — each widget is wrapped in an ErrorBoundary; shows graceful error card on failure

Phase 5 — Data Binding & Live Data (14 stories)

Wire widgets to live data sources. WebSocket subscription infrastructure. Each widget's mock data replaced with real data.

  1. [DB] Data source seeding migration — seed initial data source registry (git.timeline, git.pr-status, metrics.cycle-time, deployments.timeline, metrics.burndown, metrics.risk, metrics.gauge)
  2. [BE] Data source registry endpoint — GET /api/pagebuilder/data-sources returns available sources with schema
  3. [BE] Widget binding CRUD — POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/pagebuilder/{id}/bindings/{widgetId} to save/update/remove a binding
  4. [API] Frontend lib — listDataSources, saveBinding, deleteBinding
  5. [FE] Data binding UI — widget properties panel "Data" tab: data source dropdown, query param key-value editor, refresh interval slider
  6. [BE] WebSocket hub — Go WebSocket handler (/ws/pagebuilder/{pageId}); manages subscription state per connection
  7. [FE] WebSocket client — usePageBuilderSocket hook: connects, subscribes to widget data streams, handles reconnect + exponential backoff
  8. [FE] useDataSource hook — useDataSource(widgetId) → { data, loading, error, lastUpdate }; consumed by widgets
  9. [FE] Git Graph — live data via useDataSource; replaces mock
  10. [FE] PR Status Feed — live data via useDataSource; replaces mock
  11. [FE] Cycle Time Forecast — live data; replaces mock
  12. [FE] Deployment Timeline — live data; replaces mock
  13. [FE] Work Item List — live data; replaces mock
  14. [E2E] Data binding integration test — bind Git Graph to git.timeline, verify WebSocket message received and widget re-renders

Phase 6 — Responsive, Theme, Templates & Sharing (16 stories)

Responsive breakpoint editor, theme editor, template system (page + component templates), and export/share.

  1. [FE] Responsive toolbar — Desktop / Tablet / Mobile preview toggle in canvas toolbar; canvas viewport shrinks
  2. [FE] Breakpoint overrides in properties panel — Row column-count override per breakpoint; Column width override per breakpoint; element visibility per breakpoint
  3. [BE] Breakpoint-aware layout serialization — layout save/load preserves breakpoints object per element
  4. [FE] Breakpoint live preview — canvas reflowing elements per breakpoint selection (CSS variables swapped)
  5. [FE] Theme panel — brand colour picker, logo upload (SVG/PNG → base64), font family dropdown (Inter/Roboto/Open Sans)
  6. [BE] Theme save/load endpoint — GET/PATCH /api/pagebuilder/{id}/theme
  7. [API] Frontend lib — getTheme, patchTheme
  8. [FE] Theme live preview — canvas updates CSS variables immediately as theme changes
  9. [DB] Template tables migration — page_builder_templates (page templates) + page_builder_component_templates (widget/section templates) scoped by tenant
  10. [BE] Template CRUD — GET/POST /api/pagebuilder/templates, GET/DELETE /api/pagebuilder/templates/{id}, POST /api/pagebuilder/templates/{id}/apply
  11. [API] Frontend lib — listTemplates, saveAsTemplate, applyTemplate, deleteTemplate
  12. [FE] Save as template — "Save as Template" button on any element or page; prompts for template name + description; stores full JSON subtree
  13. [FE] Template browser — modal grid of available templates (page + component); preview thumbnail (rendered mini-canvas); click to apply
  14. [BE] Page sharing — POST /api/pagebuilder/{id}/share generates a signed read-only token; GET /api/pagebuilder/shared/{token} returns layout without auth
  15. [FE] Share modal — share button → modal with copy-link; read-only view route /pagebuilder/shared/[token]
  16. [FE] Page export — download layout as JSON; import from JSON file (validates schema before applying)

Dependency Order (Critical Path)

Phase 0 (foundation) → must complete before any other phase
Phase 1 (canvas) → depends on Phase 0
Phase 2 (properties) → depends on Phase 1
Phase 3 (palette) → depends on Phase 1 (can run in parallel with Phase 2)
Phase 4 (widgets) → depends on Phase 2 + Phase 3
Phase 5 (live data) → depends on Phase 4
Phase 6 (theme/templates/share) → depends on Phase 4 (can start parallel to Phase 5)

Feature Area & Labels

All 100 stories use feature area FE-PGB-xxxx labels (pre-allocated in the story registry). Stories are allocated IDs 00202–00301. Every card carries: AIGEN, PH-0005, FE-PGB-xxxx, EST-F#, RISK-LOW/MED/HIGH.

Architecture Deep-Dive: Container Model

Why Elementor-Style Containers?

Initial flat grid model (12-column grid, absolute positioning) limited composition flexibility. A container-based hierarchy (like Elementor) provides:

  • Nesting: Sections contain Rows, Rows contain Columns, Columns contain Widgets (leaf nodes).
  • Dynamic grid: Each Row can have 1–6 columns, customizable per row.
  • Responsive: Breakpoint-aware layout rules (desktop: 3 cols, tablet: 2 cols, mobile: 1 col).
  • Drag-to-nest: Drag widget onto column to assign; drag column to reorder within row.
  • Preset layouts: Pre-built section templates (1-col banner, 2-col dashboard, 3-col cards, 4-col grid).
  • Flex-based sizing: Column widths calculated via flex properties (grow, shrink, basis) rather than fixed pixel/grid cells.
  • Scalability: Add new container types (Tab container, Accordion) without flattening structure.

Comparison: Flat Grid vs. Elementor-Style

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Feature                │ Flat 12-col Grid  │ Elementor-Style    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Structure              │ Array of widgets  │ Nested element tree│
│ Composition           │ Overlays, gaps    │ Containers ensure  │
│                       │                    │ flow              │
│ Column count          │ Fixed 12          │ 1–N per row        │
│ Responsive            │ Manual per widget  │ Inherits via      │
│                       │                    │ breakpoints       │
│ Drag-to-nest          │ No (drag to XY)   │ Yes (drag to      │
│                       │                    │ container)        │
│ Presets               │ No                 │ Yes (1-col, 2-col,│
│                       │                    │ 3-col, 4-col)     │
│ Flex layout           │ No                 │ Yes (auto-reflow) │
│ Complexity            │ F5–F8             │ F13 (split to     │
│                       │                    │ F8+F5+F8)         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Hierarchical Element Structure

Element Types

  • Section: Top-level container. Full-width or boxed. Can have background, padding, gradient. Contains Rows.
  • Row: Horizontal container within a Section. Specifies column count (1–6+). Contains Columns.
  • Column: Individual cell within a Row. Auto-sized via flex or manual %. Contains Widgets or nested Containers.
  • Widget: Leaf node (Terminal). Renders actual content (Git graph, text, image, button, etc.). Cannot contain children. Binds to data source.

Example Page JSON (Hierarchical)

{
  "id": "page-uuid",
  "title": "Backend Team Dashboard",
  "elements": [
    {
      "id": "section-1",
      "elType": "section",
      "settings": {
        "layout": "boxed",
        "width": "1200px",
        "padding": "20px",
        "backgroundColor": "#ffffff"
      },
      "elements": [
        {
          "id": "row-1",
          "elType": "row",
          "settings": {
            "columns": 2,
            "gap": "16px",
            "verticalAlign": "stretch"
          },
          "elements": [
            {
              "id": "col-1",
              "elType": "column",
              "settings": {
                "width": "50%",
                "padding": "10px"
              },
              "elements": [
                {
                  "id": "widget-1",
                  "elType": "widget",
                  "widgetType": "git-graph",
                  "settings": {
                    "repos": ["auth", "api"],
                    "height": "400px",
                    "showBranches": true
                  },
                  "binding": {
                    "dataSource": "git.timeline.multi-repo",
                    "refreshInterval": 5000,
                    "params": { "repos": ["auth", "api"] }
                  }
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "id": "col-2",
              "elType": "column",
              "settings": {
                "width": "50%",
                "padding": "10px"
              },
              "elements": [
                {
                  "id": "widget-2",
                  "elType": "widget",
                  "widgetType": "pr-status-feed",
                  "settings": {
                    "height": "400px"
                  },
                  "binding": {
                    "dataSource": "git.pr-status.multi-repo",
                    "refreshInterval": 5000,
                    "params": { "repos": ["auth", "api"] }
                  }
                }
              ]
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "section-2",
      "elType": "section",
      "settings": {
        "layout": "full-width",
        "backgroundColor": "#f5f5f5",
        "padding": "20px 0"
      },
      "elements": [
        {
          "id": "row-2",
          "elType": "row",
          "settings": {
            "columns": 3,
            "gap": "12px"
          },
          "elements": [
            {
              "id": "col-2-1",
              "elType": "column",
              "settings": { "width": "33%" },
              "elements": [
                {
                  "id": "widget-3",
                  "elType": "widget",
                  "widgetType": "cycle-time-forecast",

                  "binding": {
                    "dataSource": "metrics.cycle-time.predictions",

                  }
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "id": "col-2-2",
              "elType": "column",
              "settings": { "width": "33%" },
              "elements": [
                {
                  "id": "widget-4",
                  "elType": "widget",
                  "widgetType": "deployment-timeline",
                  "settings": { "environment": "production" },
                  "binding": {
                    "dataSource": "deployments.timeline",
                    "refreshInterval": 10000,
                    "params": { "env": "prod" }
                  }
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "id": "col-2-3",
              "elType": "column",
              "settings": { "width": "33%" },
              "elements": [
                {
                  "id": "widget-5",
                  "elType": "widget",
                  "widgetType": "burndown-chart",
                  "settings": { "sprintId": "sprint-123" },
                  "binding": {

                    "refreshInterval": 30000,

  ],
  "theme": {
    "brandColor": "#0066cc",
    "logo": "https://...",
    "fontFamily": "Inter"
  },
  "responsiveBreakpoints": {
    "desktop": { "width": 1200 },
    "tablet": { "width": 768 },
    "mobile": { "width": 375 }
  }
}

Responsive Breakpoints

Each element can override settings per breakpoint:

{
  "id": "row-1",
  "elType": "row",
  "settings": {
    "columns": 3,  // Desktop
    "breakpoints": {
      "tablet": { "columns": 2 },
      "mobile": { "columns": 1 }
    }
  }
}

Preset Layouts

Built-in templates for rapid page creation:

  • 1-Col Banner + Content: Section (full-width banner) → Row (1 col) → Widget (text/image).
  • 2-Col Dashboard: Row (2 cols) → Columns with widgets side-by-side.
  • 3-Col Card Grid: Row (3 cols) → Columns with metric cards.
  • 4-Col Quad: Row (4 cols) → Columns with small widgets (gauges, statuses).
  • Masonry (Advanced): Row with variable-height columns (for future).

Elementor Data Structure Learnings

Analysis of Elementor's production architecture reveals key design patterns applicable to Vector PageBuilder:

  • JSON as primary format: Elementor stores all page data as JSON for human readability, easy parsing, and export/import portability. Vector should follow this pattern—all page layouts serialized as JSONB in `user_custom_page_layouts` table.
  • Three structural tiers: Elementor's data model comprises (1) General Structure (framework), (2) Page Settings (configuration), and (3) Page Content (layout + elements). Vector's model mirrors this: top-level page metadata (id, title), settings (theme, breakpoints), and content (nested element tree).
  • Responsive data handling: Elementor uses per-element breakpoint overrides in the JSON (e.g., a Row specifies columns for desktop, then overrides with fewer columns for tablet in a `breakpoints` object). Vector will adopt this same pattern for responsive flexibility without duplication.
  • Repeaters for dynamic groups: Elementor's repeater mechanism allows dynamic, repeating element groups (e.g., carousel items, list items). Vector can leverage this for future features like widget templates or dynamic data bindings that repeat across multiple repositories or teams.
  • Global styles: Elementor centralizes styling rules to avoid duplication and enable theme switching. Vector's theme editor (colors, fonts, CSS variables) should follow this principle—define once, apply globally or per-element.
  • Frontend rendering from JSON: Elementor's frontend parser interprets the JSON structure and renders the page layout. Vector's canvas and page viewer will use a similar recursive renderer: traverse the element tree (Section → Row → Column → Widget), calculate layout properties per breakpoint, and render each component.

Build Phases (5 Phases)

Phase 1: Container Editor Foundation (3 stories, 6 weeks)

Goal: Basic Section/Row/Column/Widget hierarchy with drag-to-nest and canvas rendering.

  • 📍 Canvas renders nested element tree with Sections, Rows, Columns, Widgets.
  • 📍 Drag widget to column; drag column within row; drag row within section.
  • 📍 Properties panel for Section (width, padding, bg color), Row (columns, gap), Column (width, padding), Widget (size, config).
  • 📍 Serialization of element tree to JSON.

Phase 2: Palette & Presets (2 stories, 4 weeks)

Goal: Component palette and preset layout templates.

  • 🔧 Component palette with Sections, Rows, Columns, and Widgets.
  • 🔧 Preset layout buttons (1-col, 2-col, 3-col, 4-col); drag-insert into canvas.

Phase 3: Widget Library & Data Binding (3 stories, 6 weeks)

Goal: Reusable widgets and real-time data binding system.

  • 🔌 Widget component registry (git-graph, pr-status, cycle-time, deployment, burndown, risk-summary, gauges).
  • 🔌 Data binding UI (dropdown data source, query params, refresh interval).
  • 🔌 Real-time subscription system (WebSocket or polling backend).

Phase 4: Responsive Breakpoints (2 stories, 4 weeks)

Goal: Breakpoint-aware layout rules for tablet and mobile.

  • 📱 Responsive settings editor (desktop, tablet, mobile column overrides).
  • 📱 Live preview for each breakpoint.

Phase 5: Multi-Level Aggregation & Branding (4 stories, 6 weeks)

Goal: Team/PO/exec scoping, theme editor, and page sharing.

  • 🎨 Team-level page scoping (filter by selected teams).
  • 🎨 PO aggregation view (roll-up charts, status summary).
  • 🎨 Exec health dashboard (release status, risk, forecasts).
  • 🎨 Theme editor (brand color, logo upload, font selection, CSS customization).
  • 🎨 Page export and sharing (public URL, JSON export, duplication).

User Stories & Backlog (Revised)

Note: Stories below reflect the Elementor-style container model. IDs start from 00188 (00175–00187 are allocated to Work Items feature).

Phase 1: Container Editor Foundation

Story 00188 — Canvas with Section/Row/Column Hierarchy
FE-PGB-0001, EST-F8, RISK-MED

As a team lead, I wish to see Sections, Rows, and Columns rendered on a canvas so that I can build a hierarchical page layout.

  • Canvas renders element tree (Section → Row → Columns).
  • Sections are visually distinct containers (background, padding visible).
  • Rows show column grid (2-col example: two equal-width columns with gap).
  • Columns auto-size via flex or manual width override.
  • Elements outline on hover (faint border) to show nesting structure.
  • Undo/redo support (Immer-based state, max 50 steps).

Story 00189 — Drag-to-Nest & Reflow
FE-PGB-0002, EST-F8, RISK-HIGH

As a team lead, I wish to drag widgets into columns and columns within rows so that I can compose the layout interactively.

  • Drag widget from palette or canvas → onto column → widget inserts into column's element array.
  • Drag column edge → reorder within row (update element order).
  • Drag Row → reorder within section.
  • Reflow: siblings auto-adjust widths (if column removed, remaining columns grow).
  • Visual drop zones (highlight column on hover to show drop target).
  • Drag abort (Escape key) reverts to previous state.

Story 00190 — Properties Panel for Containers & Widgets
FE-PGB-0003, EST-F5, RISK-MED

As a team lead, I wish to configure Section, Row, Column, and Widget properties in a panel so that I can customize layout and appearance.

  • Right sidebar properties panel (context-aware per selected element).
  • Section props: width (boxed/full-width), padding, background color, gap.
  • Row props: column count (1–6), gap, vertical alignment.
  • Column props: width (% or flex), padding.
  • Widget props: height, component-specific config (repos, chart type, etc.), binding (data source, params, refresh).
  • Delete button removes element and children from canvas.
  • Auto-save on blur; dirty flag tracking.

Phase 2: Palette & Presets

Story 00191 — Component Palette (Sections, Rows, Containers, Widgets)
FE-PGB-0004, EST-F3, RISK-LOW

As a team lead, I wish to drag Sections, Rows, and Widgets from a palette so that I can quickly add elements to the canvas.

  • Left sidebar palette with tabs: Containers (Section, Row, Column), Widgets (git-graph, pr-status, etc.).
  • Drag from palette → canvas inserts new instance with default settings.
  • Widget drag defaults: size 300×300px, inserts into selected column (or creates new row if needed).
  • Palette search: filter by name ("graph", "git", "metrics").

Story 00192 — Preset Layout Templates
FE-PGB-0005, EST-F2, RISK-LOW

As a team lead, I wish to insert preset layouts (1-col, 2-col, 3-col, 4-col sections) so that I can rapidly scaffold a page structure.

  • Preset buttons above palette: "1-Col", "2-Col", "3-Col", "4-Col".
  • Click → inserts new Section with Row (N columns) into canvas.
  • Each preset includes sample Column widths and light background for visual clarity.

Phase 3: Widget Library & Data Binding

Story 00193 — Widget Component Registry
FE-PGB-0006, EST-F5, RISK-MED

As a component developer, I wish to register reusable widgets with metadata so that the page builder knows how to instantiate and configure them.

  • Component registry (map of type → component factory + metadata).
  • Each widget exports: `type`, `icon`, `defaultConfig`, `configSchema` (JSON Schema).
  • Built-in widgets: git-graph, pr-status-feed, cycle-time-forecast, deployment-timeline, work-item-list, burndown-chart, risk-summary, metric-gauge.
  • Properties panel uses configSchema to render dynamic form fields per widget.

Story 00194 — Data Binding UI & Configuration
FE-PGB-0007, EST-F5, RISK-MED

As a team lead, I wish to bind widgets to data sources so that each widget pulls live data (Git timeline, PR status, metrics).

  • Widget properties panel includes "Data Source" dropdown (fetches from backend registry).
  • Query params UI (key-value editor) for filters (repos, teams, time window).
  • Refresh interval slider (1s to 5m).
  • Live preview: widget shows sample data as config changes.
  • Binding payload saved to backend (user_custom_page_bindings table).

Story 00195 — Real-Time Data Subscription
FE-PGB-0008, EST-F8, RISK-HIGH

As a team member, I wish to see live updates on widgets without page refresh so that my dashboard is always current.

  • Backend WebSocket listener (or long-poll fallback) for data source updates.
  • Frontend subscription hook: `useDataSource(sourceKey, params)` → { data, loading, error, lastUpdate }.
  • Widget receives data via hook; re-renders on update.
  • Automatic reconnect + exponential backoff on disconnect.
  • Max 5 concurrent subscriptions per page (configurable).

Phase 4: Responsive Breakpoints

Story 00196 — Responsive Settings Editor (Desktop, Tablet, Mobile)
FE-PGB-0009, EST-F5, RISK-MED

As a team lead, I wish to customize layout per breakpoint (desktop, tablet, mobile) so that my dashboard is responsive.

  • Properties panel includes "Responsive" tab with breakpoint toggles.
  • Per breakpoint: override columns (row), width (column), visibility (element).
  • Live preview: switch between desktop (1200px), tablet (768px), mobile (375px) views.

Story 00197 — Breakpoint Viewer & Preview
FE-PGB-0010, EST-F3, RISK-LOW

As a team lead, I wish to preview my page on different screen sizes so that I can verify responsive behavior.

  • Toolbar buttons: "Desktop", "Tablet", "Mobile" views.
  • Canvas viewport resizes to breakpoint width.
  • Elements reflow per breakpoint settings (column count changes, visibility toggles).

Phase 5: Multi-Level Aggregation & Branding

Story 00198 — Team-Level Page Scoping
FE-PGB-0011, EST-F3, RISK-MED

As a team lead, I wish to see only my team's work items and Git repos on my dashboard so that my page is focused.

  • Page creation includes team selection (multiselect).
  • Data bindings automatically filter by selected teams.
  • Work item list widget shows only items assigned to my team.
  • Git graph widget shows only repos owned by my team.

Story 00199 — Product Owner Aggregation View
FE-PGB-0012, EST-F8, RISK-HIGH

As a product owner, I wish to see aggregated progress across multiple teams so that I can track release health.

  • PO page template: roll-up chart (teams stacked, features by status).
  • Risk summary (RISK-HIGH count, trending).
  • Cycle time aggregates (median across teams).
  • Deployment status (teams deployed today, blockers?).
  • Data bindings query with `teams: [...]` parameter.

Story 00200 — Theme Editor (Colors, Logo, Fonts)
FE-PGB-0013, EST-F3, RISK-LOW

As a team lead, I wish to customize colors, logo, and fonts so that my page matches my team's brand.

  • Theme panel with color picker (primary, accent, neutral).
  • Logo upload (SVG/PNG, stored as base64 in JSON).
  • Font family dropdown (Inter, Roboto, Open Sans, custom).
  • Live preview on canvas.
  • Apply to all widgets or per-widget override.

Story 00201 — Page Export, Sharing & Duplication
FE-PGB-0014, EST-F3, RISK-LOW

As a team lead, I wish to export and share my page so that others can view or duplicate it.

  • Share button: generate public read-only URL.
  • Export button: download page JSON for version control.
  • Duplicate page: create copy with new name.
  • Access control: owner + shared team members.

Data Model & Schema

New Tables

CREATE TABLE user_custom_page_layouts (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  page_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES user_custom_pages(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  layout_json JSONB NOT NULL, -- Full nested element tree + theme
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
  UNIQUE(page_id) -- One layout per page
);

CREATE TABLE user_custom_page_bindings (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  page_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES user_custom_pages(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  widget_id TEXT NOT NULL, -- Element ID within layout
  data_source TEXT NOT NULL, -- e.g., "git.timeline.multi-repo"
  query_params JSONB, -- {"repos": ["auth", "api"], ...}
  refresh_interval_sec INT DEFAULT 5,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE data_sources (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  key TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, -- "git.timeline.multi-repo"
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  schema JSONB, -- JSON Schema for query_params validation
  backend_handler TEXT, -- Service that handles this source
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);

API Endpoints

-- Page layout CRUD
GET    /api/custom-pages/{id}/layout
POST   /api/custom-pages/{id}/layout
PATCH  /api/custom-pages/{id}/layout

-- Data bindings
GET    /api/custom-pages/{id}/bindings
POST   /api/custom-pages/{id}/bindings
DELETE /api/custom-pages/{id}/bindings/{binding_id}

-- Data sources (registry)
GET    /api/data-sources
GET    /api/data-sources/{key}

-- Real-time subscriptions
WS     /ws/custom-pages/{id}/data
       Send: {"subscribe": "git.timeline.multi-repo", "params": {...}}
       Recv: {"source": "...", "data": {...}, "timestamp": ...}

Widget Library (Reusable Components)

Each widget is a React component that exports:

export const GitGraphWidget = {
  type: 'git-graph',
  icon: '🌳',
  configSchema: {
    repos: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    showBranches: { type: 'boolean', default: true },
    showDeployments: { type: 'boolean', default: true },
  },
  Component: ({ data, config, isLoading, error }) => {
    // Render Git graph using visx/D3.js
  },
};

Built-in widgets:

  • git-graph — Interactive commit DAG, branches, deployments.
  • pr-status-feed — Real-time list of PRs (open, merged, blocked).
  • cycle-time-forecast — Forecast of PR merge time with confidence band.
  • deployment-timeline — Deployment history, status per environment.
  • work-item-list — Filterable list of work items.
  • burndown-chart — Sprint/feature burndown over time.
  • risk-summary — Red/yellow/green risk cards.
  • metrics-gauge — Single metric (cycle time, uptime, etc.) as a gauge.
